revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:硫黄泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:硫黄泉]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:硫黄泉]]
[[File:Tamagoyu, 天渓の湯 01.jpg|thumb|right|An example of a sulfur spring: the "Tenkei no Yu" at {{ill|Takayu Onsen|en|Takayu Onsen|WD=Q7677489|lt=Takayu Onsen}}'''Sulfur Spring''' (硫黄泉, Iōsen) is a classification of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen}}<nowiki/>based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. It is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to its unique components. == Overview == Sulfur springs are characterized by a rotten egg smell caused by {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|en|Hydrogen sulfide|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}}<nowiki/>and the frequent occurrence of cloudy water due to {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}} (mineral deposits). While the labeling system categorizes all sulfur springs under a single term, they are generally divided into two main types based on the presence or absence of hydrogen sulfide: sulfur-based springs and '''hydrogen sulfide springs.''' Identification of the type can be made using the new or old water quality labels.
The pH range of sulfur springs varies widely. Springs with higher acidity (lower pH) are typically hydrogen sulfide springs, while those with higher alkalinity are often sulfur-based. Notable high-sulfur concentration onsen in Japan include {{ill|Takayu Onsen|en|Takayu Onsen|WD=Q7677489|lt=Takayu Onsen}}(Fukushima), {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}} (Gunma), and {{ill|Tsukioka Onsen|ja|月岡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Onsen|qq=}} (Niigata). == Definition == Sulfur springs are defined as containing at least 2 mg of total sulfur (S) per kilogram of water, including HS-, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>--, and H<sub>2</sub>S.<ref>[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/point/ Definition and Protection of Onsen - Ministry of the Environment]</ref>
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == {| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfur Spring || Sulfur Spring || S Spring |- | Simple Sulfur Spring || Simple Sulfur Spring || Simple S Spring |- | Sulfur and Sodium Chloride Spring || Sulfur and Sodium Chloride Spring || S-Na-Cl Spring |- | Sulfur and Sodium Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || Sulfur and Sodium Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || S-Na-Cl･HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Hydrogen Sulfide Spring || Sulfur Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide Type) || S Spring (H<sub>2</sub>S Type) |}
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
Benefits associated with sulfur springs include the following:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * '''General indications:''' Chronic {{ill|skin diseases|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|qq=}}, chronic {{ill|gynecological disorders|en|Gynecological disorder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gynecological disorders|qq=}} , {{ill|cuts|en|Cut (wound)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cuts|qq=}} , and {{ill|diabetes|en|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diabetes|qq=}} .
* '''Hydrogen sulfide type:''' Effective for {{ill|hypertension|en|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hypertension|qq=}}<nowiki/>and {{ill|arteriosclerosis|en|Arteriosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arteriosclerosis|qq=}} ==== Drinking ==== * Beneficial for diabetes, {{ill|gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gout|qq=}}, {{ill|constipation|en|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/constipation|qq=}} , and {{ill|diarrhea|en|Diarrhea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diarrhea|qq=}} . === Contraindications === ==== Bathing ==== * '''General contraindications:''' Overly sensitive {{ill|skin|en|Human skin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin|qq=}}<nowiki/>or {{ill|mucous membranes|en|Mucous membrane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mucous membranes|qq=}}, particularly in individuals with {{ill|photosensitivity|en|Photosensitivity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/photosensitivity|qq=}} .<ref>[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/pdf/pamph_full_en.pdf Onsen_ABC]</ref> == Representative Onsen == * {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|川湯温泉 (北海道)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Hakone Yumoto Onsen|ja|箱根湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箱根温泉|1=Hakone Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q908993}}(Kanagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Nikkō Yumoto Onsen|ja|日光湯元温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}(Tochigi Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen}}(Gunma Prefecture)
* {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture)
* {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}(Nagano Prefecture)
* Ebino Kōgen Onsen (Miyazaki Prefecture)
== Accidents == Hydrogen sulfide springs can release hydrogen sulfide gas into the air, which may cause {{ill|poisoning|en|Poisoning|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poisoning|qq=}}<nowiki/>when inhaled in large concentrations or for extended periods. Proper {{ill|ventilation|en|Ventilation (architecture)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ventilation|qq=}} is essential in indoor baths. In outdoor springs or {{ill|wilderness hot springs|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}}, high-concentration hydrogen sulfide gas, which is heavier than air, tends to accumulate in depressions or hollows, potentially leading to fatal poisoning incidents.<ref group="注">Between 1935 and 1985, nine deaths occurred at {{ill|Jigokudani Onsen (Tateyama)|ja|地獄谷 (立山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigokudani Onsen (Tateyama)|qq=}} .</ref> Snow-covered onsen in winter can also trap gas, increasing the risk of poisoning.<ref>[http://www.iamp.tohoku.ac.jp/~liquid/MURA/hitorigoto/gas-kiji/h2s.html Hydrogen Sulfide Poisoning Incidents]</ref> {{Seealso|Hydrogen sulfide#Toxicity}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki}}
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{reflist|group="注"}}
=== References === {{reflist}} *

[[File:Tamagoyu, 天渓の湯 01.jpg|thumb|right|An example of a sulfur spring: the "Tenkei no Yu" at {{ill|Takayu Onsen|en|Takayu Onsen|WD=Q7677489|lt=Takayu Onsen|1=Takayu Onsen}}'''Sulfur Spring''' (硫黄泉, Iōsen) is a classification of {{ill|Onsen|en|Onsen|WD=Q655311|lt=Onsen|1=Onsen}}<nowiki/>based on the {{ill|Hot Spring Types|ja|温泉の泉質|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hot Spring Types|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} labeling system. It is categorized as a {{ill|medical spring|ja|温泉#療養泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/medical spring|1=Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q655311}} due to its unique components. == Overview == Sulfur springs are characterized by a rotten egg smell caused by {{ill|hydrogen sulfide|en|Hydrogen sulfide|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hydrogen sulfide|qq=}}<nowiki/>and the frequent occurrence of cloudy water due to {{ill|Sinter|ja|湯の花|lt=Yunohana|1=Sinter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|13=User:Immanuelle/Sinter|qq=}} (mineral deposits). While the labeling system categorizes all sulfur springs under a single term, they are generally divided into two main types based on the presence or absence of hydrogen sulfide: sulfur-based springs and '''hydrogen sulfide springs.''' Identification of the type can be made using the new or old water quality labels.
The pH range of sulfur springs varies widely. Springs with higher acidity (lower pH) are typically hydrogen sulfide springs, while those with higher alkalinity are often sulfur-based. Notable high-sulfur concentration onsen in Japan include {{ill|Takayu Onsen|en|Takayu Onsen|WD=Q7677489|lt=Takayu Onsen|1=Takayu Onsen}}(Fukushima), {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}} (Gunma), and {{ill|Tsukioka Onsen|ja|月岡温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukioka Onsen|qq=}} (Niigata). == Definition == Sulfur springs are defined as containing at least 2 mg of total sulfur (S) per kilogram of water, including HS-, S<sub>2</sub>O<sub>3</sub>--, and H<sub>2</sub>S.<ref>[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/point/ Definition and Protection of Onsen - Ministry of the Environment]</ref>
== Comparison of Old and New Labels == {| class="wikitable" |+ Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels<ref>{{Cite web|title=Reference Material: Comparison of Old and New Onsen Water Quality Labels |url=https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/docs/senshitsumei26.pdf |format=PDF |publisher=Natural Environment Bureau, Ministry of the Environment |accessdate=2018-03-26 }} </ref> ! Old Label !! New Label !! Abbreviated Label |- | Sulfur Spring || Sulfur Spring || S Spring |- | Simple Sulfur Spring || Simple Sulfur Spring || Simple S Spring |- | Sulfur and Sodium Chloride Spring || Sulfur and Sodium Chloride Spring || S-Na-Cl Spring |- | Sulfur and Sodium Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || Sulfur and Sodium Bicarbonate-Chloride Spring || S-Na-Cl･HCO<sub>3</sub> Spring |- | Hydrogen Sulfide Spring || Sulfur Spring (Hydrogen Sulfide Type) || S Spring (H<sub>2</sub>S Type) |}
== Health Benefits == ''Note: [[Health effects of hot springs|Effects]] are not guaranteed for all individuals.''
Benefits associated with sulfur springs include the following:
=== Indications === ==== Bathing ==== * '''General indications:''' Chronic {{ill|skin diseases|en|Skin disease|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin diseases|qq=}}, chronic {{ill|gynecological disorders|en|Gynecological disorder|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gynecological disorders|qq=}} , {{ill|cuts|en|Cut (wound)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/cuts|qq=}} , and {{ill|diabetes|en|Diabetes mellitus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diabetes|qq=}} .
* '''Hydrogen sulfide type:''' Effective for {{ill|hypertension|en|Hypertension|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hypertension|qq=}}<nowiki/>and {{ill|arteriosclerosis|en|Arteriosclerosis|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/arteriosclerosis|qq=}} ==== Drinking ==== * Beneficial for diabetes, {{ill|gout|en|Gout|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/gout|qq=}}, {{ill|constipation|en|Constipation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/constipation|qq=}} , and {{ill|diarrhea|en|Diarrhea|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/diarrhea|qq=}} . === Contraindications === ==== Bathing ==== * '''General contraindications:''' Overly sensitive {{ill|skin|en|Human skin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/skin|qq=}}<nowiki/>or {{ill|mucous membranes|en|Mucous membrane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mucous membranes|qq=}}, particularly in individuals with {{ill|photosensitivity|en|Photosensitivity|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/photosensitivity|qq=}} .<ref>[https://www.env.go.jp/nature/onsen/pdf/pamph_full_en.pdf Onsen_ABC]</ref> == Representative Onsen == * {{ill|Kawayu Onsen (Hokkaido)|ja|川湯温泉 (北海道)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawayu Onsen (Hokkaido)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Hakone Yumoto Onsen|ja|箱根湯本温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakone Yumoto Onsen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箱根温泉|1=Hakone Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q908993}}(Kanagawa Prefecture)
* {{ill|Nikkō Yumoto Onsen|ja|日光湯元温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nikkō Yumoto Onsen|qq=}}(Tochigi Prefecture)
* {{ill|Kusatsu Onsen|en|Kusatsu Onsen|WD=Q3137477|lt=Kusatsu Onsen|1=Kusatsu Onsen}}(Gunma Prefecture)
* {{ill|Manza Onsen|ja|万座温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manza Onsen|qq=}}(Gunma Prefecture)
* {{ill|Nozawa Onsen|ja|野沢温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nozawa Onsen|qq=}}(Nagano Prefecture)
* Ebino Kōgen Onsen (Miyazaki Prefecture)
== Accidents == Hydrogen sulfide springs can release hydrogen sulfide gas into the air, which may cause {{ill|poisoning|en|Poisoning|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/poisoning|qq=}}<nowiki/>when inhaled in large concentrations or for extended periods. Proper {{ill|ventilation|en|Ventilation (architecture)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ventilation|qq=}} is essential in indoor baths. In outdoor springs or {{ill|wilderness hot springs|ja|野湯|1=Wild Hot Springs|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wilderness hot springs|13=User:Immanuelle/Wild Hot Springs|qq=}}, high-concentration hydrogen sulfide gas, which is heavier than air, tends to accumulate in depressions or hollows, potentially leading to fatal poisoning incidents.<ref group="注">Between 1935 and 1985, nine deaths occurred at {{ill|Jigokudani Onsen (Tateyama)|ja|地獄谷 (立山町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jigokudani Onsen (Tateyama)|qq=}} .</ref> Snow-covered onsen in winter can also trap gas, increasing the risk of poisoning.<ref>[http://www.iamp.tohoku.ac.jp/~liquid/MURA/hitorigoto/gas-kiji/h2s.html Hydrogen Sulfide Poisoning Incidents]</ref> {{Seealso|Hydrogen sulfide#Toxicity}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Sessho-seki|en|Sessho-seki|WD=Q2616914|lt=Sessho-seki|1=Sessho-seki}}
== Notes ==
=== Annotations === {{reflist|group="注"}}
=== References === {{reflist}} *
